player_notes.kevin durant: OUT for Game 6 vs. Lakers with ankle injury. Massive role expansion expected for Rockets supporting cast — Jabari Smith Jr., Tari Eason, and others should see increased usage/minutes.
player_notes.austin reaves: Returned from a 9-game injury absence in Game 5 vs. Rockets. Monitor minutes restriction risk in Game 6; workload after extended absence warrants caution on high prop lines.
player_notes.brandon ingram: Exited Game 5 vs. Cavaliers with right heel inflammation. Status uncertain for Game 6 — if out, significant usage opens up for Raptors teammates.

game_notes.HOU vs LAL: Kevin Durant (OUT, ankle) misses Game 6. Rockets' offensive load redistributes to Smith Jr., Eason, and others — target their points/rebounds props. Reaves back for Lakers but monitor minutes ceiling after long absence.
game_notes.TOR vs CLE: Brandon Ingram left Game 5 with right heel inflammation; availability for Game 6 TBD. If he's out, Raptors' remaining players absorb significant shot and usage share.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Durant's status (listed DOUBT per injury report) remains the critical HOU variable — HOU has now won G5 without him... G6 key: Reaves' health and conditioning trajectory after his first game back are the primary LAL variables... and Durant's ankle availability is the decisive HOU structural variable that could transform this series outcome regardless of the current 3-2 scoreline.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Multiple sources (ESPN, Shams Charania) confirm Kevin Durant will NOT play in Game 6 vs. Lakers due to the ankle injury. This moves him from DOUBT/questionable to confirmed OUT for G6.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Durant's Game 6 status from DOUBT to confirmed OUT. Remove any conditional framing around his G6 availability. HOU must again rely on the Thompson-Sengun-Smith offensive structure for G6.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Game 5 diary entry (Apr 29) documents Banchero 45/9/7 and Ausar Thompson 6/15/6 — Wagner is not mentioned as absent or injured in the G5 diary.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News confirms Franz Wagner was OUT for Game 5 vs. Pistons with a strained right calf. The season context G5 diary does not reflect his absence, which is a significant ORL roster/injury omission.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Franz Wagner to the ACTIVE WITH DOCUMENTED CONCERNS or CONFIRMED OUT section with right calf strain. Update the DET vs ORL G5 diary to note Wagner was OUT (strained right calf), which affected ORL's rotation and context for all G5 stat lines. Flag his availability for G6 as uncertain.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Context states Reaves returned in G5 (Apr 29): 'played 34 min, 22/4/6 on 4-16 FG — ahead of the stated Charania timeline.' Series score listed as LAL leads 3-2 after G5 (Apr 29) with HOU winning 99-93.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News confirms the G5 final score was HOU 99, LAL 93 (series now 3-2 LAL) and that Reaves returned. The context already reflects the correct score, but the news headline 'Jabari Smith Jr. scored 22 points, Tari Eason added 18' introduces Tari Eason as a significant HOU contributor (18 points) who is not mentioned in the G5 diary at all.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update the LAL vs HOU G5 diary to include Tari Eason's 18-point contribution, which represents an emerging HOU secondary scoring option not previously documented in the series diary.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: G5 diary (Apr 29) credits Harden 23/9/5 and Mobley 23/9/1 as CLE's key contributors. Mitchell 19/5/3 noted. No mention of Dennis Schroder as a significant contributor.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News reports that 'Dennis Schroder scored 11 of his 19 points in the fourth quarter' and was described as breaking out in Game 5, framed as a key factor in CLE's 125-120 win. The G5 diary omits Schroder's 19-point performance entirely.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update the CLE vs TOR G5 diary to include Schroder's 19-point breakout (11 in Q4), which represents an important emerging CLE bench contributor and changes the G6 tactical picture.
